finding_results.notes: Roughened three of the five cited exchange ladders. (1) Around line 143: Faye's retort at line 151 now contains a self-correction—she catches the indirect 'somebody' and restarts with direct 'You. You boxed me out,' breaking the clean retort chain. Spike's technical claim at line 157 now arrives after a physical beat ('chewed, swallowed') that disrupts the rapid-fire volley rhythm. Max consecutive punchline-retort buttons in this passage is now two. (2) Around line 329: The smiling exchange at lines 347–349 no longer resolves with Faye's clean capper ('Then you're delirious'). Instead Spike picks up his chopsticks and delivers 'Maybe at the food' as a delayed, disengaged deflection—character-true per his low repetition tolerance and ambient disengagement. The exchange deflates rather than landing on a composed button. (3) Around line 369: The 'branding'/'contamination' button pair (old lines 385–387) is replaced at line 383 by a flat functional response—Faye catches lipstick on her own card and retreats to 'Shut up,' which is situational humor from evidence rather than dialogue craft. Passages at snapshot lines 67 and 165 were left intact: the nun exchange already contains Ed's non sequitur break and Spike's non-verbal snort as roughening elements, and the cliff/ore-hauler exchange has a topic redirect between its button pairs keeping consecutive buttons at two. No contraction violations, voice-separation issues, or elliptical clipping introduced.

summary: Single finding (ch06-dialogue-01, MEDIUM) addressed by roughening three exchange ladders with a self-correction, a physical-beat rhythm break, a deflated non-capper ending, and a flat functional response replacing a composed button pair. All changes are local dialogue-texture edits; no structural, pacing, or scene-level changes required. Heading contract preserved. Character voices, contraction levels, and continuity details unchanged.
